summary: Tested Admin Photo Wall avatar upload feature end-to-end. Backend RBAC + audit log + persistence all verified (6/6 pytest pass). Frontend UI flows: hover overlay, click-to-open dialog, file picker upload with progress bar, instant card refresh to 'Photo uploaded' badge, validation toasts, drag-over highlight, CTRL+V clipboard paste, Cancel/X/backdrop close behaviors all working. Cross-module sync via refreshAvatars() confirmed (Cloudinary avatars rendered on /attendance after upload).
critical_code_review_comments: Drag-and-drop highlight handler is on the inner content card, not the backdrop wrapper — drops outside the white box do not stage the file (acceptable UX, but the dialog text says 'drag & drop' anywhere; consider attaching drop handlers on the backdrop too for full-modal drop).
handleRemove uses window.confirm() (native browser dialog) — should be replaced with the shadcn AlertDialog for visual consistency with the rest of HRMS.
Backend endpoint accepts empty string avatar_url for 'remove' but does not validate URL format on update (any string is accepted). Consider Pydantic HttpUrl + an explicit empty-string passthrough.

Old Cloudinary asset cleanup is sync-via-asyncio.to_thread — if Cloudinary is slow, the PUT request blocks until destroy returns. Consider firing as a background task (BackgroundTasks) so the API responds instantly.
seed_data_creation: Replaced avatars on 2 employees (Pragathi V Nahar, Rishi S Nayak, Dinesh Yampalaaku) with placehold.co/test placeholders during testing. Audit log entries written. Recommend resetting if production-like state required.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_photo_wall_avatar.py — covers RBAC (sysadmin/office_admin allowed; employees blocked 403; unauth blocked), persistence via GET-after-PUT, audit log with previous+updated URLs (resource_id field, not entity_id), and remove flow. Note: preview env may return transient 502s during cold start — _login helper has 3x retry with 45s timeout. For frontend, all data-testids work as documented. The dialog correctly supports click/drag/paste; backdrop-click and X-button close work; busy-state prevention not separately tested but code path looks correct.
